author: klim0v2652
content:
Hi, could you explain how in lighthouse v8.1.x I can check the root for the duties of the proposers of the next epoch?
Previously, I expected an event.block conforming to this specification: 
https://ethereum.github.io/beacon-APIs/#/Validator/getProposerDuties
> Request beacon node to provide all validators that are scheduled to propose a block in the given epoch. Duties should only need to be checked once per epoch, however a chain reorganization could occur that results in a change of duties. For full safety, you should monitor head events and confirm the dependent root in this response matches:
> 
> event.current_duty_dependent_root when compute_epoch_at_slot(event.slot) == epoch
> event.block otherwise

author: sproul
content:
There should be no differences in the /v1/proposer_duties endpoint compared to before Fulu. We reverted the changes we had made to use the correct root, so we are continuing to use the wrong root as per the spec and every other client. The dependent root in the head events should agree with this (although IMO the head event may need some changes)

The upcoming /v2/proposer_duties API will use the correct dependent root for Fulu, but we haven’t released it yet.

author: sproul
content: It you’re using the current APIs you can just pretend that the dependent root Lighthouse gives you for the next epoch is correct, but it will change frequently

author: sproul
content: We’ll add /v2/proposer_duties in the next release

author: sproul
content: That should address the frequent change issue (by using the correct root post Fulu)

author: klim0v2652
content: What I see now is that the root of the previous block is used for the dependent_root of the next epoch. And for the current epoch, it's not CurrentDutyDependentRoot or PreviousDutyDependentRoot...

author: sproul
content: Yeah sorry just waking up. Yes that is a problem with the /v1/ endpoint. Prior to Fulu this was correct and the spec for /v1/ did not get updated to fix this because the issue was found too late

author: sproul
content: You can either just call the endpoint and ignore the root (it is wrong)

author: sproul
content:
To be clear: with /v2/ there will be agreement between:

- `head_event.current_duty_dependent_root` at epoch `N` and
- `dependent_root` from `/eth/v2/validator/duties/proposer/{N + 1}`

In other words, "current dependent root" from the head event is now actually for the next epoch. We could rename it, but that would be another breaking change, so probably not.
